title: javascript part 2
content:
Perfect 👍 ye bada content hai, to hum line-by-line detailed Hinglish notes banayenge — jaise teacher samjhata hai.

Main Part 1 se start karta hoon. Tum “proceed” likhoge to next part bhejunga.

📘 DOM + JavaScript Notes (Hinglish) — Part 1

🔹 DOM kya hota hai? (Basic Understanding)

👉 DOM = Document Object Model

Simple language me:

  • Jo bhi HTML page tum banate ho

  • Wo objects ka structure hota hai

Example:

  • <html> → object

  • <head> → object

  • <body> → object

  • <div> → object

👉 Ye sab milke ek tree structure banate hain
👉 Isi ko bolte hain DOM

🔹 Important Concept

👉 “Agar tum DOM samajh gaye”:

To tum:

  • Data read kar sakte ho

  • Value change kar sakte ho

  • New elements add kar sakte ho

  • Elements delete kar sakte h

🔹 Window Object (Sabse Top Level)

👉 Browser me sab kuch ek global object ke andar hota hai:

  • Uska naam hai → window

💡 Simple samjho:

  • Browser = Window

  • Sab kuch uske andar hai

🔹 Structure Start hota hai yahan se

Hierarchy:

Window
  ↓
Document
  ↓
HTML
  ↓
Head + Body

🔹 Window ke andar kya hota hai?

Jab tum console me likhte ho:

console.log(window)

👉 Tumhe bahut saari cheezein dikhti hain:

  • location (tum kaha ho)

  • history

  • navigator

  • document

👉 Ye sab window ke properties hain

🔹 Important: document

👉 Sabse important object hai:

document

👉 Ye represent karta hai:

  • Tumhara pura HTML page

🔹 Shortcut Understanding

  • window.document ✔️

  • document ✔️ (direct use kar sakte ho)

👉 Kyun?

  • Kyunki browser automatically samajhta hai ki tum document use karoge

🔹 Document ke andar kya hota hai?

console.log(document)

👉 Tumhe milta hai:

  • doctype

  • html

  • head

  • body

👉 Ye hi actual DOM structure hai

🔹 DOM Tree ka Concept (Very Important 🔥)

👉 DOM ek tree structure hota hai

Structure:

Window
  ↓
Document
  ↓
HTML
  ├── Head
  │     ├── Title
  │     │     └── Text Node
  │     └── Meta (attributes only)
  │
  └── Body
        └── Div
              ├── H1
              │     ├── Attribute (Class name)
              │     └── Text Node
              │
              └── Paragraph
                    └── Text Node

🔹 Node kya hota hai?

👉 DOM me har cheez ko Node bola jata hai

Types:

  1. Element Node
    → <div>, <h1>, <p>

  2. Text Node
    → Jo text likha hai

  3. Attribute Node
    → class, id

🔹 Example Breakdown

<title>DOM Learning</title>

  • Title → Element Node

  • “DOM Learning” → Text Node

🔹 Parent-Child Concept

Example:

  • Div → Parent

  • H1 & P → Children

👉 Important for DOM navigation

🔹 Sibling Concept

Example:

  • H1 aur P
    👉 Dono same level pe hain → siblings

🔹 Real Use Case

Agar tumhe kisi element tak jana hai:

👉 Tumhe path pata hona chahiye:

window → document → html → body → div → p

🔹 Selection ka logic

Agar multiple div ho:

👉 Tumhe identify karna padega:

  • class

  • id

  • attribute

📘 DOM + JavaScript Notes (Hinglish) — Part 3

🔹 Real Browser me DOM kaise dekhein?

👉 Kisi bhi website pe jao
👉 Right click → Inspect

👉 Fir open karo:

  • Console tab

Observation

👉 Jab tum window open karte ho:

  • Bahut zyada data hota hai

👉 Jab tum document open karte ho:

  • Focused HTML structure milta hai

🔹 Advanced Console Methods 

DOCUMENT PURE PAGE KO PKD KE RKHTA H 

Normal log:

console.log(document)

👉 Limited view deta hai

Better debugging:

console.dir(document)

👉 Full properties show karta hai

🔹 Useful Properties

1. Base URL

document.baseURI

👉 Current page ka URL deta hai

2. Links Collection

document.links

👉 Page ke saare links deta hai

🔹 Important Concept ⚠️

👉 document.links kya return karta hai?

  • ❌ Array nahi

  • ✅ HTMLCollection

🔹 HTMLCollection vs Array

👉 Difference:

FeatureHTMLCollectionArray
map()❌✅
forEach()❌✅
index access✅✅

🔹 Example

document.links[0]

👉 First link mil jayega

🔹 Conversion Trick

👉 Agar array banana ho:

Array.from(document.links)

👉 Ab tum map, filter use kar sakte ho

🔹 Element Select kaise karein?

👉 DOM ka main kaam = elements select karna

🔹 Method 1: getElementById ⭐

document.getElementById("firstHeading")

👉 Ye return karega:

  • pura HTML element

Example output:

<h1 id="firstHeading">...</h1>

🔹 Ab Manipulation start 🔥

👉 Ab hum element change kar sakte hain

🔹 innerHTML (Very Important)

document.getElementById("firstHeading").innerHTML = "Chai aur Code"

👉 Result:

  • Page ka heading change ho jayega

🔹 Kya hua internally?

👉 Tumne:

  • DOM access kiya

  • Element select kiya

  • Text replace kar diya

👉 Isko bolte hain:

👉 DOM Manipulation

🔹 Real Power 💥

Ab tum kar sakte ho:

  • Text change

  • HTML add/remove

  • Elements create

  • Elements delete                                  

🔹 Console Me Kaam Start

  • Browser me Console open kiya

  • Yahin se hum JavaScript DOM ke saath interact karenge

🔹 Element Select Karna (getElementById)

  • Sabse pehla method:

document.getElementById("title")

👉 Breakdown:

  • document → pura HTML page

  • .getElementById() → specific element ko select karega

  • "title" → id jo humne diya tha

⚠️ Important Syntax Note

  • Case sensitive hota hai:

    • getElementById (G, E, I capital)

🔹 Output Kya Mila?

  • Ye method return karta hai:
    👉 poora HTML element (object form me)

🔹 Object Se Data Kaise Nikaale?

  • Dot (.) use karke properties access karte hain

Example:

document.getElementById("title").id

👉 Output: "title"

🔹 Class Access Karna

document.getElementById("title").className

👉 Important:

  • HTML me → class

  • JavaScript me → className

⚠️ Concept Clear

  • JS me directly class nahi likhte

  • Always use:
    👉 className

🔹 getAttribute() Method

  • Kisi bhi attribute ki value lene ke liye:

document.getElementById("title").getAttribute("id")

👉 Output: "title"

document.getElementById("title").getAttribute("class")

👉 Output: "heading"

🔹 setAttribute() Method

  • Attribute change/set karne ke liye

document.getElementById("title").setAttribute("class", "test")

👉 Result:

  • Old class "heading" remove ho jayegi

  • New class "test" set ho jayegi

⚠️ Important Warning

  • setAttribute overwrite karta hai

  • Old value remove ho jaati hai

🔹 Multiple Classes Set Karna

document.getElementById("title").setAttribute("class", "test heading")

👉 Result:

  • Dono classes apply ho jayengi

🔹 Variable Me Store Karna

  • Easy access ke liye:

const title = document.getElementById("title")

👉 Ab baar-baar likhne ki zarurat nahi

🔹 Key Learning

  • Element mil gaya → ab usko:

    • read kar sakte ho

    • modify kar sakte ho

    • attributes change kar sakte ho

📒 Part 3 Notes (Style Manipulation + Styling via JS) – Hinglish

🔹 Element Already Stored

const title = document.getElementById("title")

👉 Ab title ek object hai jiske andar bahut properties hain

🔹 Style Property Access Karna

title.style

👉 Ye ek object hai jisme saari CSS properties hoti hain

🔹 CSS vs JS Syntax Difference

  • CSS me:

background-color

  • JS me:

backgroundColor

👉 camelCase use hota hai JS me

🔹 Background Color Change

title.style.backgroundColor = "green"

👉 Instant UI change ho jayega

🔹 Text Color Change

title.style.color = "white"

🔹 Padding Add Karna

title.style.padding = "15px"

🔹 Border Radius Add Karna

title.style.borderRadius = "10px"

🔹 Important Concept

  • style ek object hai

  • Har CSS property ko:
    👉 .propertyName = "value" se set karte hain

🔹 Live Change Effect

  • Jaise hi code run karte ho:
    👉 Browser me instant change visible

🔹 Multiple Styles Apply Karna

title.style.backgroundColor = "green"
title.style.color = "white"
title.style.padding = "15px"
title.style.borderRadius = "10px"

🔹 Key Learning

  • JS se styling karna = dynamic UI change

  • Ye hi real power hai DOM manipulation ki

📒 Part 4 Notes (Content Manipulation – textContent, innerText, innerHTML) – Hinglish

🔹 Content Nikalna (Text Access)

  • Element ka actual text lene ke liye:

title.textContent

👉 Output: pura text jo element ke andar hai

🔹 Similar Properties (3 Important)

  1. textContent

  2. innerText

  3. innerHTML

👉 Teeno similar lagte hain but difference hai ⚠️

🔸 1. textContent

title.textContent

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • Saara content de deta hai

  • Even hidden content bhi (CSS se hide ho toh bhi)

🔸 2. innerText

title.innerText

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • Sirf visible text deta hai

  • Jo screen pe dikh raha hai wahi milega

🔸 3. innerHTML

title.innerHTML

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • HTML ke saath content deta hai

  • Tags bhi include karta hai

🔹 Example Situation

  • Suppose:

<h1 id="title">
  Hello <span style="display:none;">Hidden</span>
</h1>

🔹 Difference समझो

  • textContent 👉 "Hello Hidden"

  • innerText 👉 "Hello"

  • innerHTML 👉 "Hello <span style='display:none;'>Hidden</span>"

🔹 Key Concept

  • textContent → full data (hidden + visible)

  • innerText → only visible

  • innerHTML → HTML structure bhi

🔹 Content Change Karna

👉 Text change kar sakte ho:

title.innerText = "New Text"

🔹 HTML Inject Karna

title.innerHTML = "<h2>New Heading</h2>"

👉 Ye:

  • pura content replace karega

  • new HTML render karega

⚠️ Important Warning

  • innerHTML dangerous ho sakta hai:

    • agar user input directly daal diya toh
      👉 XSS attack ho sakta hai

🔹 Real Use Cases

  • textContent → safe data fetch

  • innerText → UI-based changes

  • innerHTML → HTML insert karna

✅ Summary (Part 4)

  • 3 main properties samjhi

  • Hidden vs visible difference clear hua

  • Content read + change karna seekha

  • HTML insert karna bhi seekha

📒 Part 5 Notes (Query Selector + Advanced Selection) – Hinglish

🔹 Problem with Old Methods

  • getElementById → sirf id ke liye

  • getElementsByClassName → limited
    👉 Real-world me flexible nahi

  • This can do:

    • multiple classes

    • nested elements

    • attribute selectors

    • pseudo-classes

🔹 Solution: querySelector 🔥

document.querySelector("h1")

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • First matching element return karta hai

🔹 Important Rule

  • Ye hamesha:
    👉 sirf first element select karta hai

🔹 Multiple Elements Example

<h2>One</h2>
<h2>Two</h2>
<h2>Three</h2>
document.querySelector("h2")

👉 Output: "One" (first h2)

🔹 ID Select Karna (CSS Style)

document.querySelector("#title")

👉 # → id selector

🔹 Class Select Karna

document.querySelector(".heading")

👉 . → class selector

🔹 CSS Selectors Use Karna

👉 Same CSS syntax use hota hai:

document.querySelector("div p")

👉 matlab:

  • div ke andar jo p hai → select karo

🔹 Attribute Selector

document.querySelector("input[type='password']")

👉 specific element select kar sakte ho

🔹 querySelectorAll 🔥

document.querySelectorAll("h2")

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • saare matching elements deta hai

🔹 Return Type

  • querySelector → single element

  • querySelectorAll → NodeList

🔹 NodeList kya hota hai?

  • Array jaisa hota hai

  • But exact array nahi hota

🔹 NodeList Access

const items = document.querySelectorAll("h2")

items[0]  // first element
items[1]  // second element

🔹 Loop Use Karna (forEach)

items.forEach(function(item) {
    item.style.color = "red"
})

👉 Saare elements pe style apply ho jayega

🔹 Real Example (List)

const ul = document.querySelector("ul")
const li = ul.querySelector("li")

li.style.backgroundColor = "green"

🔹 Chain Query (Important)

  • Parent se child select kar sakte ho:

ul.querySelector("li")

🔹 Text Change

li.innerText = "Five"

👉 Content change ho gaya

🔹 Key Learning

  • querySelector = powerful + flexible

  • CSS selectors use kar sakte ho

  • querySelectorAll = multiple elements handle

📒 Part 6 Notes (NodeList vs HTMLCollection + Array Conversion) – Hinglish

🔹 Ab Tak Kya Dekha?

  • querySelectorAll → NodeList देता है

  • getElementsByClassName → HTMLCollection देता है

👉 Dono same nahi hote ⚠️

🔸 NodeList kya hota hai?

const items = document.querySelectorAll("li")

👉 Features:

  • Array jaisa hota hai

  • Index se access kar sakte ho

  • forEach support karta hai ✅

items.forEach(item => {
    item.style.color = "red"
})

🔸 HTMLCollection kya hota hai?

const items = document.getElementsByClassName("list-item")

👉 Features:

  • Array jaisa dikhta hai

  • But:
    ❌ forEach directly support nahi karta
    ❌ map bhi nahi

🔹 Problem Example

items.forEach(item => {
    item.style.color = "green"
})

👉 ❌ Error aayega (HTMLCollection me forEach nahi hota)

🔸 Solution → Convert into Array 🔥

const arr = Array.from(items)

👉 Ab:

  • Pure array ban gaya

  • Saare methods use kar sakte ho

🔹 Loop Ab Kaam Karega

arr.forEach(item => {
    item.style.backgroundColor = "orange"
})

🔸 Important Difference (Simple Language)

FeatureNodeListHTMLCollection
forEach✅ Yes❌ No
map❌ No❌ No
Array jaisa✅✅
Direct loop easy✅❌

🔹 Key Concept

👉 Jab bhi:

  • querySelectorAll → easy (NodeList)

  • getElementsByClassName → convert karna padega

🔸 Real Mistake (Common)

❌ Log directly HTMLCollection pe loop laga dete hain
👉 Error aata hai

✔️ Correct way:

Array.from(collection).forEach(...)

🔹 Example Flow

const tempClassList = document.getElementsByClassName("list-item")

const convertedArray = Array.from(tempClassList)

convertedArray.forEach(li => {
    li.style.color = "blue"
})

🔹 Deep Insight

  • NodeList aur HTMLCollection:
    👉 pure arrays nahi hote

  • Isliye:
    👉 kuch methods missing hote hain

🔹 Best Practice

  • Modern JS me:
    👉 querySelectorAll prefer karo

  • Agar old method use kiya:
    👉 convert to array

📒 Part 7 Notes (Real Website Practice – Wikipedia DOM Manipulation) – Hinglish

🔹 Real Practice Start

  • Ab hum apni file se bahar nikle

  • Real website pe practice ki 👉 Wikipedia

👉 Reason:

  • Real-world DOM structure samajhna

  • Complex elements handle karna

🔹 Inspect Element

  • Kisi bhi heading pe right click → Inspect

  • Example:

    • Heading (h2, h3) select ki

🔹 All Headings Select Karna

document.querySelectorAll("h2")

👉 Output:

  • NodeList of all h2 elements

🔹 Variable Me Store

const headings = document.querySelectorAll("h2")

🔹 First Element Access

headings[0].innerText

👉 Example output:

  • "History"

  • "Trademark"

🔹 Multiple Elements Manipulate Karna

👉 Loop lagate hain:

headings.forEach(function(h) {
    h.style.color = "red"
})

👉 Result:

  • Saare headings red ho gaye 🔴

🔹 Background + Padding Add

headings.forEach(function(h) {
    h.style.backgroundColor = "green"
    h.style.padding = "10px"
})

🔹 Content Change Karna

headings.forEach(function(h) {
    h.innerText = "Hitesh"
})

👉 Result:

  • Saare headings ka text change 😄

🔹 Important Observation ⚠️

  • Kabhi extra elements bhi select ho jaate hain
    👉 Kyun?

  • Same tag (h2) multiple jagah use hota hai

🔹 Better Targeting (Class Use)

document.querySelectorAll(".mw-headline")

👉 Ye specific class hai Wikipedia headings ki

🔹 Store + Loop

const heads = document.querySelectorAll(".mw-headline")

heads.forEach(function(h) {
    h.style.color = "black"
    h.style.backgroundColor = "green"
    h.style.padding = "5px"
})

🔹 Key Learning

  • Real websites me:
    👉 exact selector choose karna important hai

  • Sirf tag se kaam nahi chalta
    👉 class/id better hota hai

🔹 Powerful Concept

👉 Jo humne console me kiya:

  • Same code hum:

    • <script> tag me likh sakte hain

    • ya external JS file me

🔹 Real Use Case

  • Database se data aaya

  • Loop lagaya

  • Existing elements update kiye

👉 Ye sab same concept se hota hai

✅ Summary (Part 7)

  • Real website pe DOM practice ki

  • Multiple elements select kiye

  • forEach se manipulate kiya

  • Styling + content change kiya

  • Correct selector choose karna seekha

🎯 FINAL UNDERSTANDING:

  • Element select karo

  • Loop lagao (agar multiple ho)

  • Style / content change karo

👉 यही है DOM manipulation ka core 💯

🔥 Agar chaho to next mai main tumhe:

  • Pure revision cheat sheet

  • ya practice questions + mini project

bhi de sakta hoon 👍

continue

📒 Part 8 Notes (Final Wrap + Real Understanding + Mindset) – Hinglish

🔹 Ab Tak Kya Seekha (Big Picture)

👉 DOM manipulation ka pura flow:

  1. Element select karo

  2. Usko variable me store karo

  3. Uski properties access karo

  4. Style / content change karo

🔹 Core Methods Recap

  • getElementById

  • querySelector

  • querySelectorAll

  • getElementsByClassName

👉 Inhi se 90% kaam ho jata hai

🔹 Core Properties Recap

  • .style → CSS change

  • .innerText → visible text

  • .textContent → full text

  • .innerHTML → HTML ke saath

🔹 Important Thinking Pattern 🧠

👉 Har problem me socho:

  • Kya mujhe single element chahiye?
    → querySelector

  • Kya mujhe multiple elements chahiye?
    → querySelectorAll

🔹 Loop Use Karna

👉 Jab multiple elements ho:

elements.forEach(el => {
    el.style.color = "blue"
})

🔹 Real World Use Case

👉 Suppose:

  • Database se 10 items aaye

  • Already 10 <li> hain page pe

👉 Tum kya karoge?

  • querySelectorAll("li")

  • loop lagao

  • data fill karo

🔹 Important Concept (Very Important ⚠️)

👉 JavaScript console me jo kiya:

  • Same cheez:

    • <script> tag me likh sakte ho

    • ya .js file me

🔹 Mistakes Jo Sab Karte Hain ❌

  1. Wrong selector use karna

  2. HTMLCollection pe direct loop lagana

  3. innerHTML misuse karna

  4. Case sensitivity ignore karna

🔹 Best Practices ✅

  • Prefer querySelector & querySelectorAll

  • Clean selectors use karo (class/id)

  • Loop properly use karo

  • Debug using console

🔹 Powerful Realization 💡

👉 Frameworks (React, Angular, etc.):

  • Internally yehi sab karte hain

👉 Isliye:

  • JS DOM strong = future safe

🔹 Final लाइन (Golden Line ⭐)

👉
"Agar tum element select karna samajh gaye, to tum DOM control kar sakte ho."

✅ Final Summary (Part 8)

  • Complete DOM flow clear hua

  • Methods + properties revise ki

  • Real-world thinking develop hui

  • Common mistakes avoid karna seekha

🎯 Ab tum kya kar sakte ho:

  • Apni website me dynamic changes

  • Data ko UI me inject karna

  • Basic projects banana
